events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need analyze scenario: classified area wiring methods, missing seal-off fitting at boundary. NEC 500-516 covers hazardous locations. Seal-offs required at boundaries between classified and unclassified areas for conduit systems to prevent passage of gases/vapors. Missing seal-off is non-compliant and potentially high risk (explosion hazard). Work stage rough-complete. Decision fail. Component conditions: installed-defective? It's missing, so non-compliant. Maybe installed-defective because rough-in incomplete/defective. Need choose categories that apply. The schema says component_conditions may apply: installed-defective, non-compliant. Missing component means non-compliant; could also installed-defective because work is roughed in but not complete. We'll include both. Risk high or critical? Missing seal-off in classified area boundary is serious explosion hazard; could be critical. But maybe high. Need decide. In industrial classified area, seal-off is critical safety feature. Missing at boundary is critical risk. However schema risk options include critical. Let's set critical. Percent complete maybe 80? Rough-complete but missing seal means not complete. Could be 75. Value 0. Sound etc irrelevant 0/empty. Findings: missing seal-off fitting at boundary; actions: install approved seal-off fitting per NEC/ drawings, inspect and test. Rationale: NEC requires seals at boundaries to prevent migration of flammable atmospheres; absence violates 501.15/502.15/503.15/505.16 etc. References: NEC 500-516. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

  "findings": [
    "Missing seal-off fitting at the classified-area boundary.",
    "Seal-off is required to prevent propagation of flammable gases/vapors/dusts through the wiring system into unclassified areas or between classified zones."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install the approved seal-off fitting at the classified-area boundary as shown on the drawings and per the NEC.",
    "Verify correct sealing compound/fiber filling and proper placement (within required distance of boundary).",
    "Re-inspect the circuit before close-out and energization."
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC Articles 500–516 require seals at classified-location boundaries for conduit and cable systems. A missing seal-off creates a path for hazardous atmospheres to migrate, defeating the explosionproof/dust-ignitionproof wiring method and presenting an ignition/explosion risk. The installation is therefore non-compliant and must be corrected before acceptance.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 500-516",
    "NEC 501.15 (Class I seals)",
    "NEC 502.15 (Class II seals)",
    "NEC 503.15 (Class III seals)",
    "NEC 505.16 (Zone 0, 1, 2 seals)"
  ]
}
